description: FEAT-006 decomposition 4/4 + FEAT-011 launch-chain integration (UNIFIED 2026-05-28 per user direction). Closes FEAT-006 north-star end-to-end loop AND closes the launch-chain canonical-intention gaps (FGAP-117/118/120/121/122/123 from the 2026-05-28 audit at commit 945dc93). The two were scope-merged because FEAT-011's launch-script enhancement (META wiring + skill-narrative + generate-skills.js update + per-target config.tool_operations[] read + --grant flag + pre-flight pointer check + TraceEntry observability + docs cascade + hygiene cascade) IS the same launch-script work TASK-091 needs for the FEAT-006 demo. Building both as one task = single coordinated implementation. SCOPE: (a) wire pi-agent-dispatch into META + skills-gen + own-package self-registration (FGAP-117 closure); (b) author packages/pi-agent-dispatch/skill-narrative.md + add pi.skills field + skills/ in files[] + generate-skills.js packageDirs[] addition; (c) build scripts/orchestrator/launch-constrained-orchestrator.ts (or enhance scripts/launch-constrained-pi.sh in-place — implementation plan-mode decides) that: spawns pi -p with all 4 extensions loaded; reads target's config.tool_operations[] at launch + extends --tools allowlist with declared composite canonical_ids; offers --grant <composite-id> flag (repeatable) to scope to subset; pre-flight pointer-presence check warns on absent .pi-context.json; tool surface restricted (substrate-write + call-agent + author-agent-spec + run-real-checks + commit-attested + author-tool-grant + declared composites — no raw bash, no raw file-write per DEC-0014); (d) build packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/work-order-loop.ts implementing bounded iteration with human-OK gate per DEC-0047 governance; (e) composite-loader emits TraceEntry warning + pi.ui.notify when loadContext returns config:null (FGAP-121 closure layer-a); (f) docs cascade: launch script header refresh (4 extensions + canon-anchor citations) + packages/pi-project-workflows/README.md refresh + NEW docs/launching-the-orchestrator.md orientation doc (FGAP-122 closure); (g) hygiene cascade: pre-launch SKILL.md presence verification + features.json status flips (FEAT-004 proposed→in-progress; FEAT-005 proposed→complete since FEAT-010 closure covers it; FEAT-011 in-progress→complete on this TASK-091's completion) (FGAP-123 closure); (h) runtime demonstration committed at analysis/2026-05-28-feat-006-end-to-end-demo.md exercises one complete loop iteration end-to-end against a real LLM: orchestrator authors a concrete work-order; dispatches via call-agent with bounded grant; agent makes the edit; real-checks pass (build/check/test exit 0); attested-commit lands with writer.kind=agent footer; report captures the work-order content + dispatch invocation + agent output + real-check result + commit SHA + attestation footer. Closes FEAT-006 north-star + closes FGAP-102 formally (production demo lands). Out of scope (separate follow-on TASK): FGAP-119 dogfood composite authoring via author-tool-grant invocations from inside the constrained pi session — requires this TASK-091 already merged so author-tool-grant is reachable.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/orchestrator/launch-constrained-orchestrator.ts (or enhanced scripts/launch-constrained-pi.sh — implementation plan decides) invokes pi -p with: pi-context + pi-jit-agents (library import path) + pi-workflows + pi-behavior-monitors + pi-agent-dispatch extensions loaded; restricts in-pi orchestrator's tool surface to substrate-write + call-agent + author-agent-spec + run-real-checks + commit-attested + author-tool-grant + declared composites (no raw bash, no raw file-write per DEC-0014); pinned non-agentic model + cost-control per F-006

work-order-loop.ts implements iteration logic: dispatch call-agent → on completion run-real-checks → on pass commit-attested; on fail capture structured fail-report + feed back to next iteration; max iterations bounded (configurable, default 3); each iteration boundary admits a human-OK gate per DEC-0047 (orchestrator pauses for human approval to continue iterating)
Loop respects DEC-0014: the in-pi orchestrator NEVER holds raw bash or file-write itself — every code change flows through call-agent → privileged agent → real-check gate → attested-commit

Loop respects DEC-0047: capability widening (granting the privileged agent more operations mid-loop) is human-only via author-tool-grant invocation; the orchestrator (writer.kind=agent at this layer) cannot widen the agent's grant

Runtime demonstration (committed at analysis/2026-05-28-feat-006-end-to-end-demo.md) exercises one complete loop iteration end-to-end against a real LLM: orchestrator authors a concrete work-order (e.g. 'add a one-line comment to packages/pi-jit-agents/src/types.ts:1'); dispatches via call-agent with bounded grant (file-write-scoped to that one file); agent makes the edit; real-checks pass (build/check/test exit 0); attested-commit lands with writer.kind=agent footer; report captures the work-order content + dispatch invocation + agent output + real-check result + commit SHA + attestation footer

packages/pi-project-workflows/package.json carries @davidorex/pi-agent-dispatch in dependencies[] + ./agent-dispatch-extension.ts in pi.extensions[]; packages/pi-project-workflows/agent-dispatch-extension.ts exists as one-line re-export shim

packages/pi-agent-dispatch/package.json carries pi.skills field + skills/ in files[]; packages/pi-agent-dispatch/skill-narrative.md exists with YAML frontmatter + XML-tagged sections per pi skill standard documenting the 5 static tools + composite-loader mechanism

scripts/generate-skills.js packageDirs[] includes 'pi-agent-dispatch'; after npm run skills: packages/pi-agent-dispatch/skills/pi-agent-dispatch/SKILL.md exists with <tool name=> tags for all 5 static tools

Launch script reads target dir's config.tool_operations[] at launch time + extends --tools allowlist with declared composite canonical_ids; --grant <composite-id> flag (repeatable) scopes which declared composites to surface; default (no --grant): expose ALL declared composites

composite-loader emits TraceEntry warning (via trace-writer pipeline per DEC-0002 pattern) + surfaces via pi.ui.notify at startup when loadContext returns config:null; launch script adds pre-flight `test -f "$TARGET/.pi-context.json" || warn` step

Launch script header refreshed: 4 extensions + canon-anchor citations (DEC-0014/0044/0047/FEAT-006/010); packages/pi-project-workflows/README.md refreshed (absorbs pi-agent-dispatch); NEW docs/launching-the-orchestrator.md orientation doc covers prerequisites + harness intention + composite-vocabulary author cadence + .pi/-touching behavior documented

Launch script adds pre-launch SKILL.md symmetry verification (count ≥ 4 extensions emitted SKILL.md OR explicit operator warning); features.json status cascade applied (FEAT-004 proposed→in-progress; FEAT-005 proposed→complete; FEAT-011 in-progress→complete on this TASK-091 completion; FEAT-006 proposed→complete on runtime-demo green)
Per DEC-0048: bundled .workflow.yaml + bundled agents NOT preserved/aligned/fixed; engine unit tests + the runtime demo + adversarial probe are the gates

VALIDATION = real checks per DEC-0018: build/check/test exit codes; runtime demo as above; adversarial probe by fresh-context Explore (verifies: in-pi orchestrator tool surface contains zero raw-code-execution surfaces; agent cannot escape grant; real-check gate cannot be bypassed; attested-commit writer.kind=agent footer present + correct; human-OK gate enforced; --grant flag scopes correctly; pre-flight pointer check fires; TraceEntry warnings emit; all 4 extensions emit SKILL.md; no parallel ungated paths; zero forbidden-term hits in code OR commit messages). NO LLM self-report at any gate (FGAP-102 / DEC-0047 clause 5)
notes: Filed 2026-05-26 + UNIFIED 2026-05-28 (absorbs FEAT-011 scope per user direction). Fifth (final) of four+ FEAT-006 decomposition tasks PLUS the launch-chain integration that the 2026-05-28 audit (commit 945dc93) surfaced as required for FEAT-006 to be ACTUALLY reachable from a launched session. Closes FEAT-006 north-star + FGAP-102 formally + FGAP-117/118/120/121/122/123 + FEAT-011 (which is the launch-chain integration scope merged into this task). FGAP-119 (dogfood composites) stays as separate follow-on TASK post-this-merge. FEAT-011 status flipped to in-progress as part of Phase 1 substrate prep (implicit enactment via user direction to build).
